title:
Title: Built an AI futures trading bot - $103 to $165 in 3 days (+60%). Here's what I learned.

Been working on an AI-powered crypto futures bot for the past week. Started with $103 on Binance and it's now sitting at ~$165 with open positions still in profit.
author: aitradingsuite6
selftext:
**he Setup:**

* Trades BTC, ETH, SOL perpetual futures
* Uses EMA crossover + RSI + VWAP + ATR for signals
* Multi-timeframe analysis (5m primary, 1m for entry precision)
* 50x leverage with strict risk management

**Risk Management (the real secret):**

* Tiered take-profit: closes 50% at +2%, 30% at +4%, remaining at +6%
* Trailing stop-loss that locks in breakeven after +2%
* Auto leverage reduction after 3 consecutive stop-losses
* Daily loss limit of 10% → full shutdown
* No new positions during low-liquidity hours (midnight-6am UTC)

**What I've learned so far:**

1. Moving stop-loss to breakeven early is a game-changer. My biggest winner (ETH LONG +$20.73) would've been a loss without it.
2. Correlated assets can hurt you - went long SOL because ETH was pumping, SOL dumped instead. Now the bot filters for correlation.
3. Weekend markets are brutally slow. Need separate parameters for weekends.
4. 50x leverage sounds crazy but with 0.8% stop-loss, max risk per trade is \~40% of position. Still aggressive but calculated.

**Current positions:**

* BTC SHORT from $72,512 → now $70,960 (floating +$8.85)
* SOL SHORT from $90.7 → now $87.6 (floating +$9.89)

The bot runs on a $6/month VPS. I open-sourced a lite version if anyone wants to try it:

`npx skills add quantum-grid-trading`

For the full version with 50x leverage and advanced features, I have a Telegram bot: u/aitradingsuite_bot

Happy to answer questions about the strategy or implementation. Still iterating on it daily.

中文版标题: 用AI做合约交易，$103 三天变 $165（+60%），分享我的策略思路

（内容同上，翻译即可）

comments.author: Any_Spring_8602
comments.body: This is exciting initial results, but a few important cautions: 3 days is an extremely small sample size that doesn't prove the strategy's viability (luck/market conditions can dominate short timeframes), and 50x leverage on a small account means one bad trade could wipe you out entirely. I'd suggest backtesting over years of data, stress-testing through bear markets, and only risking what you can afford to lose before scaling up.

replies.author: No-Consequence-1779
replies.body: Yes, it actually has trended up recently so if you didn’t make profits …

comments.author: Any_Spring_8602
comments.body: Great first results, but I'd caution that 3 days is too short to validate strategy performance — especially with 50x leverage on futures. Consider backtesting over multiple market conditions (bull, bear, sideways) and forward-testing for at least 1-2 months before scaling. Also watch for overfitting to recent market conditions, and make sure your risk management protects against black swan moves.

comments.author: shajurzi
comments.body: the botification of this entire post and comments, mistakes in title and all is hilarious.

comments.author: bjxxjj
comments.body:
Nice run, congrats on the green start — but I’d be careful drawing strong conclusions from 3 days of data, especially on 50x.

A few genuine questions since this is where most futures bots break:

- What’s your average R:R *after fees and funding*? On 50x, fees + slippage can quietly eat a big chunk of that tiered TP structure.
- How does it perform in high chop vs strong trend? EMA/RSI/VWAP combos tend to get shredded in low-volatility ranges.
- Is the 10% daily cutoff based on backtesting or psychological protection?

The tiered TP + BE trail at +2% is smart — it reduces tail risk. But at 50x, even small wicks can invalidate otherwise solid structure. Have you tested the same logic at 10–20x to compare volatility-adjusted returns?

$103 → $165 is great, but the real test is 100+ trades across different regimes. If you’re serious about validating it, I’d track:

- Max drawdown
- Sharpe/Sortino
- Win rate vs avg loss
- Performance during funding spikes

Curious to see how it holds up over a month. Keep us posted.

replies.author: aitradingsuite6
replies.body:
This will be updated daily. The current best version I'm using yields over 20% profit daily.



The maximum stop-loss is 15%, but the probability is low because the position size is small, yet it consistently makes money.



I have several other versions; this is the best one I've written, and I've started implementing it again. Today is the third day, so it seems small.



Those who understand will definitely understand.



Thank you for your support.

comments.author: ddchbr
comments.body:
Don’t understand this:

> ⁠Moving stop-loss to breakeven early is a game-changer. My biggest winner (ETH LONG +$20.73) would've been a loss without it.

How did this help facilitate your “biggest winner” if it means your BE SL got hit? At best you hit 2 out of 3 of your take profit levels and then were stopped out. Is that what happened?

comments.author: dogazine4570
comments.body:
Nice work getting it live and actually sharing the numbers. A 60% move in 3 days is eye-catching, but I’d be careful about drawing conclusions from such a small sample size — especially on 50x.

A few things I’d be curious about:

- Have you backtested this across different volatility regimes (low chop vs. high trend)? EMA + RSI + VWAP can look great in trending conditions but get shredded in sideways markets.
- What’s your max historical drawdown in backtests or paper trading?
- Are you factoring in funding rates and fees at 50x? On lower timeframes those can eat more than people expect.
- How does the bot behave during sudden wicks/liquidity grabs? 1m entries + high leverage can get slipped hard.

I do like the structured risk controls — especially the daily loss cap and auto leverage reduction after consecutive losses. That’s the part most people skip. The tiered TP + breakeven lock is solid for reducing emotional interference.

If you’re serious about validating it, I’d focus less on short-term % gain and more on:
- 100+ trade sample
- Risk-adjusted return (Sharpe/Sortino)
- Max drawdown vs. average return per trade

3 days proves it can win. The real test is whether it survives 3 months.

Keep us updated — would be interesting to see how it performs through a choppy week.
